title: OSI, TCP/IP, Encapsulation, and Traffic Evidence · Protocol Clinic: Net Tcp Udp State
summary: Trace a client request through host, switch, router, firewall, service, and return path using captures and diagrams. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net tcp udp state increment in a layered packet-flow diagnosis. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare TCP reliability and state with UDP datagrams and select transport behavior from application requirements.
Distinguish MAC, IP, port, name, and application identifiers by scope, lifetime, and forwarding purpose.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
